name: 114 Scott Ave
tagline: Historic warehouse party corridor. Dead-end near Morgan Ave L.
vibe: Graffiti warehouses, shipping containers, open paved lot. The Brooklyn warehouse party corridor (2005–2018).
setup: Against warehouse wall, facing the dead end. Sound directed away from Varick Ave.
exit: Multiple routes — Morgan Ave L under 10 min, Metropolitan Ave and Flushing Ave also accessible.
why: Best transit, best zoning, most event-friendly corridor. Area has a long history of warehouse events and the surrounding community is accustomed to activity here.
risks: Foot traffic near Morgan Ave increases visibility. Sits on the 90th/83rd precinct boundary. Ongoing development in the corridor is reducing available lots.

name: Red Hook Waterfront
tagline: Dead-end streets at the waterfront. Manhattan skyline + Statue of Liberty.
vibe: Fenced industrial lots, waterfront dead-ends. Pioneer Works corridor energy.
setup: Sullivan St preferred over Wolcott — better dispersal toward Van Brunt in multiple directions.
exit: Sullivan has multiple routes toward Van Brunt St. Wolcott dead-ends at the water with a single exit — less flexible.
why: Best views on the list. Pioneer Works sets precedent for outdoor events in this corridor. Community is culturally receptive.
risks: No subway — ferry and B61 bus only, which limits attendance naturally. Some streets dead-end at the waterfront. Active coastal construction through 2028 in the surrounding area.

name: Grand St / Newtown Creek
tagline: Industrial waterfront along Newtown Creek. Loading docks provide natural acoustic reinforcement.
vibe: Warehouses, light manufacturing. Three-sided sheds and loading docks for natural shade + sound.
setup: Use loading dock structures for shade and acoustic reinforcement. Set back from Grand St for less visibility.
exit: Grand St is a major thoroughfare with strong dispersal in all directions.
why: Excellent transit, strong dispersal options, and loading dock structures that provide natural shade and acoustic containment.
risks: Grand St has more through-traffic than the other dead-end locations, increasing visibility. Adjacent to the Newtown Creek Superfund site, though set back from the waterline. Sits near the 83rd/104th precinct boundary.

name: 151 Apollo St
tagline: Dead-end at Newtown Creek waterfront. Manhattan skyline views.
vibe: Industrial warehouses at a dead-end. Manhattan skyline views from the waterfront edge.
setup: Waterfront lots at end of Apollo St. Battery-powered equipment only — avoid fuel handling near remediation sites.
exit: Single exit route back up Apollo to Meeker Ave. Most constrained dispersal on the list.
why: Strong views, heavy manufacturing zoning keeps foot traffic low. Viable option if other locations are unavailable.
risks: Within the Greenpoint oil spill remediation zone — subsurface contamination is being managed but does not pose a surface-level concern for a short outdoor gathering. Single exit route limits flexibility. 94th Precinct has a smaller coverage area and faster response times than the 83rd. Environmental monitoring groups are active along the creek.
